source_info.section: Discussion

population: Patients with infection admitted to the emergency department (sepsis cohort)
cohort_age: Median age: 70 (56–80) in lactate group 1 and 66 (55.5–77) in lactate group 2
cohort_gender: Female: 43.05% (502/1166) in lactate group 1 and 42.92% (300/699) in lactate group 2
performance: Superior prognostic accuracy compared to qSOFA; similar predictive validity to SOFA
notes: Lactate was positively associated with qSOFA/SOFA scores and prognosis. Lactate was an independent prognostic predictor by Cox regression.

source_info.section: Discussion

population: Patients with infection admitted to the emergency department (sepsis cohort)
cohort_age: Median age: 70 (56–80) in lactate group 1 and 66 (55.5–77) in lactate group 2
cohort_gender: Female: 43.05% (502/1166) in lactate group 1 and 42.92% (300/699) in lactate group 2
notes: SOFA predictive validity was similar to serum lactate. Combining lactate with qSOFA yielded predictive ability close to SOFA alone.

source_info.section: Discussion

population: Patients with infection admitted to the emergency department (sepsis cohort)
cohort_age: Median age: 70 (56–80) in lactate group 1 and 66 (55.5–77) in lactate group 2
cohort_gender: Female: 43.05% (502/1166) in lactate group 1 and 42.92% (300/699) in lactate group 2
performance: AUROC lower than that found in Ho's study; far less sensitivity but similar specificity compared to lactate and SOFA
notes: qSOFA showed capacity to predict mortality but with lower AUROC than previously reported. Combining lactate with qSOFA improved predictive ability close to SOFA alone.

source_info.section: Discussion

cohort_age: Median age: 70 (56–80) in lactate group 1 and 66 (55.5–77) in lactate group 2
cohort_gender: Female: 43.05% (502/1166) in lactate group 1 and 42.92% (300/699) in lactate group 2
method: ROC analysis / sensitivity and specificity assessment
notes: qSOFA was compared to lactate for predicting mortality; lactate described as more stable than qSOFA when sedation is used

source_info.section: Discussion

cohort_age: Median age: 70 (56–80) in lactate group 1 and 66 (55.5–77) in lactate group 2
cohort_gender: Female: 43.05% (502/1166) in lactate group 1 and 42.92% (300/699) in lactate group 2
performance: Reasonably sensitive; more stable than qSOFA when sedation is used
notes: Lactate levels correlated significantly with SOFA scores; high lactate considered a warning signal for organ dysfunction

source_info.section: Discussion

population: Adult septic patients in the ICU
cohort_age: Median age: 70 (56–80) in lactate group 1 and 66 (55.5–77) in lactate group 2
cohort_gender: Female: 43.05% (502/1166) in lactate group 1 and 42.92% (300/699) in lactate group 2
timing: First lactate measurement after sepsis onset
notes: Retrospective study; approximately half of adult sepsis patients excluded due to lack of laboratory testing; time between first lactate measurement and sepsis onset was inconsistent; data collected from different types of ICUs introducing treatment heterogeneity

source_info.section: Discussion

population: ICU patients (CCU, CSRU, MICU, SICU, TSICU)
cohort_age: Median age: 70 (56–80) in lactate group 1 and 66 (55.5–77) in lactate group 2
cohort_gender: Female: 43.05% (502/1166) in lactate group 1 and 42.92% (300/699) in lactate group 2
performance: AUROC reported per ICU type (see supplementary figures/tables)
notes: Study compared predictive performance of lactate, qSOFA, and SOFA for mortality across different ICU types using MIMIC database. Specific AUROC values not extractable from this excerpt.
